Patch Set 4:

Could anyone give this patch a secondary review? Thank.
Patch Set 4: Verified+1 Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4: Verified-1 Code-Review-1

I found a way to crash LNet with this patch. Try these module parameters:

options ko2iblnd timeout=100 credits=2560 ntx=5120 peer_credits=63 concurrent_sends=63 fmr_pool_size=1280 fmr_flush_trigger=1024 map_on_demand=64

and then modprobe lnet;lctl net up

You will then see the following back trace:

Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.292250] LNetError: 20000:0:(o2iblnd_cb.c:1309:kiblnd_reconnect_pee
r()) ASSERTION( peer->ibp_connecting == 1 ) failed:
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.303363] LNetError: 20000:0:(o2iblnd_cb.c:1309:kiblnd_reconnect_pee
r()) LBUG
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.310739] Pid: 20000, comm: kiblnd_connd
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.314891]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.314892] Call Trace:
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.318939]  [<ffffffffa0740875>] libcfs_debug_dumpstack+0x55/0x80 [li
bcfs]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.325962]  [<ffffffffa0740e77>] lbug_with_loc+0x47/0xb0 [libcfs]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.332212]  [<ffffffffa08431c8>] kiblnd_reconnect_peer+0x118/0x150 [k
o2iblnd]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.339608]  [<ffffffffa083aee0>] kiblnd_destroy_conn+0x4c0/0x810 [ko2
iblnd]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.346784]  [<ffffffffa08486b1>] kiblnd_connd+0xc1/0xbc0 [ko2iblnd]
Dec 30 15:02:05 spoon17.ccs.ornl.gov kernel: [  337.353248]  [<ffffffff81064d00>] ? default_wake_function+0x0/0x20

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Will patchset 4 it works fine for me.  My preference is still the same with the LASSERT to prevent possible future bugs.

Patch Set 5:

James I think you are correct that ibc_reconnect needs to be set back to 0 in the cases where things can't connect but in your patch set you also moved the ibc_reconnect back to the other location in the structure.  I believe you are getting stale data for that ibc_reconnect bit triggering the problem which I why Liang moved it in the first place.

Patch Set 6:

The last patch Liang pushed I had no issues with but I didn't test the case where MOD was invalid which you correctly set ibc_reconnect=0 to prevent an infinite loop.  The conn race is a little surprising to me.  I would think at some point they would fall out of sync and not end up racing, but I'm testing at small scale (< 50 nodes).

parents.subject: LU-7623 Add __user to seq_write buffer arguments

commit.subject: LU-7569 o2iblnd: multiple fixes for reconnection
commit.message:
LU-7569 o2iblnd: multiple fixes for reconnection

This patch includes a few fixes:

- all reconnection attempts are submitted by connd now, otherwise
  o2iblnd can still generate long zombie connection list if
  there is a cmid in TIMEWAIT state on the remote side.

- move the reconnect code in kiblnd_destroy_conn() to a new function
  kiblnd_reconnect_peer(). Also, fixes the condition of submitting
  reconnection request.

- peer::ibp_max_frags and peer::ibp_queue_depth should be protected
  by glock

- code cleanup for kiblnd_reconnect(), and rename it to
  kiblnd_check_reconnect() because it will not resubmit the
  reconnection request anymore.

- ibc_reconnect should not share the same byte with ibc_schedule
  and ibc_ready, because they can be updated by kiblnd_scheduler
  and kiblnd_cq_completion which could be called even for rejected
  connection.

Signed-off-by: Liang Zhen <liang.zhen@intel.com>
Change-Id: I65afcda69d7cfa152e9dbcc9e3c7559e039bcaa2
